pattern: The requirement that a subtraction stay positive means the smallest value in the array can never borrow from anything, so its own parity fixes the only achievable target; every larger element then needs a smaller odd value available to flip toward that target.
hints: Only nums1[i] - nums1[j] with nums1[j] < nums1[i] is allowed, so whichever value is the smallest in the whole array can never use subtraction -- it must contribute its own parity as-is, and that parity becomes the only possible target.

Process the remaining values from smallest to largest. Any value whose parity already matches the target is free; any value whose parity doesn't match needs some strictly smaller value that is odd (subtracting an odd value is exactly what flips parity).
Keep a running flag for whether an odd value has appeared yet among the smaller values you've processed -- if a mismatched value shows up before any odd value has appeared, the whole array fails.

edge_inputs.description: smallest value is odd but a later mismatched value has no smaller odd helper
smallest value is even; a mismatched odd value appears before any odd helper exists
smallest value is odd, later values can all borrow from it
values at the maximum allowed magnitude

stress.description: random distinct values across the full allowed range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    nums1 = rng.sample(range(1, 1000000001), n)
    return [nums1]
